questions.question: What is the nature of God's existence?

options.text: God is a finite being with limitations
God is an impersonal force
God is the infinite, eternal, unchangeable Spirit
God is a concept created by humans

questions.question: How is God's sovereignty understood in relation to human free will?

options.text: God's sovereignty overrides all human choices
Human free will operates completely outside of God's control
God's sovereignty and human free will are compatible mysteries
God does not exercise control over creation

questions.question: How many divine persons exist within the Godhead?

options.text: Only one person, the Father
Three distinct persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
Two persons: Father and Son
Many gods exist

questions.question: What is God's relationship to time?

options.text: God exists within time like creation
God is completely outside of time
God is both in time and outside of time
Time does not exist for God

questions.question: How is God's omniscience understood?

options.text: God knows all things past, present, and future
God knows all possibilities but not all future certainties
God learns and grows in knowledge
God's knowledge is limited to what is logical

questions.question: What is the nature of Christ's person?

options.text: Christ was merely a human teacher
Christ only appeared to be human
Christ is fully God and fully human
Christ was a created being

questions.question: What was the primary purpose of Christ's atonement?

options.text: To demonstrate God's love without a substitutionary element
To pay a ransom to Satan
To satisfy God's justice by bearing the penalty for sin
To restore moral order in the universe

questions.question: How is Christ's resurrection understood?

options.text: Christ's resurrection was merely spiritual
Christ's body was stolen and the resurrection was a hoax
Christ physically rose from the dead with a glorified body
The resurrection stories are myths with symbolic meaning

questions.question: What is Christ's current role and ministry?

options.text: Christ is inactive until His return
Christ serves as High Priest, Mediator, and Advocate
Christ's work was completed at the cross
Christ has been absorbed back into the Father

questions.question: How will Christ's second coming be understood?

options.text: Christ has already returned spiritually
Christ's return will be physical, visible, and unexpected
Christ's return is symbolic of the gospel's triumph
Christ will return after the millennium

questions.question: How are the three persons of the Trinity related to each other?

options.text: The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are three modes of one person
The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are three separate gods
The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are distinct persons in one divine essence
The Son and Spirit are lesser divine beings than the Father

questions.question: How is the Holy Spirit related to the Father and Son?

options.text: The Holy Spirit is a force or influence, not a person
The Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father only
The Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father and the Son
The Holy Spirit is a created being

questions.question: How is the Son related to the Father?

options.text: The Son is a created being
The Son is eternally begotten, not made
The Son is identical to the Father
The Son is subordinate to the Father in essence

questions.question: How do the persons of the Trinity work together?

options.text: Each person works independently of the others
The persons take turns being active
The persons work inseparably in all divine actions
Only the Father acts, while the Son and Spirit are passive

questions.question: How is the Trinity revealed in Scripture?

options.text: The Trinity is explicitly taught throughout Scripture
The Trinity is a later theological development not found in Scripture
The Trinity is progressively revealed and implicit in Scripture
The Trinity contradicts the clear teaching of Scripture

questions.question: What is the nature of the Church?

options.text: The Church is merely a human organization
The Church is a spiritual organism composed of all true believers
The Church is a visible institution with hierarchical authority
The Church is irrelevant to true spirituality

questions.question: What is the purpose of church government?

options.text: Church government should follow a hierarchical structure with bishops
Church government should be led by elders with congregational input
Church government should be democratic with the congregation making decisions
Church government is unnecessary and should be avoided

questions.question: What are the sacraments/ordinances of the Church?

options.text: There are seven sacraments that convey grace
There are two ordinances (Baptism and Lord's Supper) as symbols
There are two sacraments that convey grace to believers
There are no sacraments or ordinances required

questions.question: What is the relationship between the Church and Israel?

options.text: The Church has completely replaced Israel in God's plan
Israel and the Church remain distinct entities in God's plan
The Church is the expansion of Israel to include Gentiles
Israel has no future role in God's plan

questions.question: What is the mission of the Church?

options.text: The Church's mission is primarily social justice
The Church's mission is to make disciples of all nations
The Church's mission is to preserve tradition
The Church's mission is to provide personal spiritual experiences

questions.question: What is the origin of sin?

options.text: Sin originated with Adam's disobedience in the Garden
Sin is an inherent part of human nature
Sin is the result of improper social conditions
Sin is an illusion or misunderstanding

questions.question: What is the extent of sin's impact on humanity?

options.text: Humans are morally weakened but not corrupted by sin
Every aspect of human nature is corrupted by sin
Humans retain the ability to choose good without divine help
Sin only affects certain aspects of human nature

questions.question: How is sin transmitted from generation to generation?

options.text: Sin is transmitted through natural generation
Each soul is created by God but inherits a sinful nature
Sin is learned through environment and example
Sin is not transmitted but chosen individually

questions.question: What is the relationship between sin and human will?

options.text: Humans have complete freedom to choose good or evil
Human will is in bondage to sin but can respond to God's grace
Human will is completely determined by God
Human will is an illusion

questions.question: What is the ultimate consequence of sin?

options.text: Sin leads to temporal consequences but not eternal punishment
Sin leads to eternal separation from God in hell
Sin will ultimately be forgiven for all people
Sin's consequences can be overcome through human effort

questions.question: What is the nature of human beings?

options.text: Humans are purely physical beings
Humans are composed of body and soul/spirit
Humans are composed of body, soul, and spirit
Humans are purely spiritual beings temporarily inhabiting bodies

questions.question: What is the image of God in humanity?

options.text: The image of God is primarily physical resemblance
The image of God consists of rational and moral capacities
The image of God is relational capacity
The image of God was completely lost in the Fall

questions.question: What is the purpose of human existence?

options.text: Humans exist to pursue personal happiness
Humans exist to glorify God and enjoy Him forever
Humans exist to achieve self-actualization
Humans exist to serve as caretakers of creation

questions.question: What is the relationship between body and soul in human nature?

options.text: The body is a prison for the soul
Body and soul are distinct but integrally related
Body and soul are identical
The body is evil and the soul is good

questions.question: What is the destiny of human beings?

options.text: All humans will ultimately be saved
Humans will either enjoy eternal life with God or eternal punishment
Humans will cease to exist at death
Humans will be reincarnated until they achieve perfection
